You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.173 (commit: 93f4312a).

## Issue to Resolve

Add `Show play buttons on cards with audio` preference as in Anki Desktop
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Anki Desktop has a feature called  `Show play buttons on cards with audio`, but AnkiDroid doesn't have it

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Add the pref to AnkiDroid


**Additional context**
![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/69634269/37388d7f-7313-4ce6-86f9-9f04d228dddf)

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aankitects%2Fanki%20hide_audio_play_buttons&type=code

https://github.com/ankitects/anki/blob/e327195470824074465657f98bb0282c7c93a56e/qt/aqt/main.py#L587-L592

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/AbstractFlashcardViewer.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/analytics/UsageAnalytics.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/HtmlGenerator.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/preferences/AppearanceSettingsFragment.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/SoundKt.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/preferences.xml`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/xml/preferences_appearance.xml`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 17→package com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer
 18→
 19→import android.content.Context
 20→import android.content.res.Resources
 21→import androidx.annotation.CheckResult
 22→import com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 23→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.ReviewerCustomFonts
 24→import com.ichi2.libanki.Card
 25→import com.ichi2.libanki.Sound
 26→import timber.log.Timber
 27→import java.io.BufferedReader
 28→import java.io.IOException
 29→import java.io.InputStream
 30→import java.io.InputStreamReader
 31→
 32→class HtmlGenerator(
 33→    private val typeAnswer: TypeAnswer,
 34→    val cardAppearance: CardAppearance,
 35→    val cardTemplate: CardTemplate,
 36→    val resources: Resources
 37→) {
 38→
 39→    @CheckResult
 40→    fun generateHtml(card: Card, reload: Boolean, side: Side): CardHtml {
 41→        return CardHtml.createInstance(card, reload, side, this)
 42→    }
 43→
 44→    fun filterTypeAnswer(content: String, side: Side): String {
 45→        return when (side) {
 46→            Side.FRONT -> typeAnswer.filterQuestion(content)
 47→            Side.BACK -> typeAnswer.filterAnswer(content)
 48→        }
 49→    }
 50→
 51→    fun expandSounds(content: String): String {
 52→        return Sound.expandSounds(content)
 53→    }
 54→
 55→    companion object {
 56→        fun createInstance(
 57→            context: Context,
 58→            typeAnswer: TypeAnswer
 59→        ): HtmlGenerator {
 60→            val preferences = context.sharedPrefs()
 61→            val cardAppearance = CardAppearance.create(ReviewerCustomFonts(), preferences)
 62→            val cardHtmlTemplate = loadCardTemplate(context)
 63→
 64→            return HtmlGenerator(
 65→                typeAnswer,
 66→                cardAppearance,
 67→                cardHtmlTemplate,
 68→                context.resources
 69→            )
 70→        }
 71→
 72→        /**
 73→         * Load the template for the card
 74→         */
 75→        private fun loadCardTemplate(viewer: Context): CardTemplate {
 76→            try {
 77→                val data = convertStreamToString(viewer.assets.open("card_template.html"))
 78→                return CardTemplate(data)
 79→            } catch (e: IOException) {
 80→                Timber.w(e)
 81→                throw RuntimeException(e)
 82→            }
 83→        }
 84→
 85→        /**
 86→         * Converts an InputStream to a String.
 87→         *
 88→         * @param input InputStream to convert
 89→         * @return String version of the InputStream
 90→         */
 91→        private fun convertStreamToString(input: InputStream?): String {
 92→            var contentOfMyInputStream = ""
 93→            try {
 94→                val rd = BufferedReader(InputStreamReader(input), 4096)
 95→                var line: String?
 96→                val sb = StringBuilder()
 97→                while (rd.readLine().also { line = it } != null) {
 98→                    sb.append(line)
 99→                }
100→                rd.close()
101→                contentOfMyInputStream = sb.toString()
102→            } catch (e: Exception) {
103→                Timber.w(e)
104→            }
105→            return contentOfMyInputStream
106→        }
107→    }
108→}
109→

See the GNU General Public License for more details. 12→ * 13→ * You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along with 14→ * this program. If not, see . 15→ * 16→ * This file incorporates code under the following license 17→ * https://github.com/ankitects/anki/blob/2.1.34/pylib/anki/sound.py 18→ * 19→ * Copyright: Ankitects Pty Ltd and contributors 20→ * License: GNU AGPL, version 3 or later; http://www.gnu.org/licenses/agpl.html 21→ */ 22→ 23→@file:Suppress("unused") 24→ 25→package com.ichi2.libanki 26→ 27→import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager.withCol 28→import com.ichi2.utils.KotlinCleanup 29→ 30→@KotlinCleanup("combine file with Sound.kt if done in libAnki") 31→/** 32→ * Records information about a text to speech tag. 33→ */ 34→data class TTSTag( 35→ val fieldText: String, 36→ /** 37→ * Language may be empty if coming from AnkiDroid reading the whole card 38→ */ 39→ val lang: String, 40→ val voices: List, 41→ val speed: Float, 42→ /** each arg should be in the form 'foo=bar' */ 43→ val otherArgs: List 44→) : AvTag() 45→ 46→/** 47→ * Contains the filename inside a [sound:...] tag. 48→ * 49→ * Video files also use [sound:...]. 50→ */ 51→data class SoundOrVideoTag(val filename: String) : AvTag() 52→ 53→/** In python, this is a union of [TTSTag] and [SoundOrVideoTag] */ 54→open class AvTag 55→ 56→/* Methods */ 57→ 58→val AV_REF_RE = Regex("\\[anki:(play:(.):(\\d+))]") 59→val AV_PLAYLINK_RE = Regex("playsound:(.):(\\d+)") 60→ 61→fun stripAvRefs(text: String) = AV_REF_RE.replace("", text) 62→ 63→fun addPlayIcons(content: String): String { 64→ return AV_REF_RE.replace(content) { match -> 65→ val groups = match.groupValues 66→ val side = groups[2] 67→ val index = groups[3] 68→ val playsound = "playsound:$side:$index" 69→ """ 70→ 72→ """ 73→ } 74→} 75→ 76→/** Extract av tag from playsound:q:x link */ 77→suspend fun getAvTag(card: Card, url: String): AvTag? { 78→ return AV_PLAYLINK_RE.matchEntire(url)?.let { 79→ val values = it.groupValues 80→ val questionSide = values[1] == "q" 81→ val index = values[2].toInt() 82→ val tags = withCol { 83→ if (questionSide) { card.questionAvTags() } else { card.answerAvTags() } 84→ } 85→ if (index < tags.size) { 86→ tags[index] 87→ } else { 88→ null 89→ } 90→ } 91→} 92→

 17→package com.ichi2.anki.analytics
 18→
 19→import android.content.Context
 20→import android.content.SharedPreferences
 21→import android.os.Build
 22→import android.webkit.WebSettings
 23→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 24→import androidx.core.content.edit
 25→import com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.GoogleAnalytics
 26→import com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.GoogleAnalyticsConfig
 27→import com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.httpclient.OkHttpClientImpl
 28→import com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.request.DefaultRequest
 29→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidApp
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.BuildConfig
 31→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 32→import com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 33→import com.ichi2.utils.DisplayUtils
 34→import com.ichi2.utils.KotlinCleanup
 35→import com.ichi2.utils.WebViewDebugging.hasSetDataDirectory
 36→import org.acra.ACRA
 37→import org.acra.util.Installation
 38→import timber.log.Timber
 39→
 40→@KotlinCleanup("see if we can make variables lazy, or properties without the `s` prefix")
 41→object UsageAnalytics {
 42→    const val ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY = "analyticsOptIn"
 43→
 44→    @KotlinCleanup("lateinit")
 45→    private var sAnalytics: GoogleAnalytics? = null
 46→    private var sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler: Thread.UncaughtExceptionHandler? = null
 47→    private var sOptIn = false
 48→    private var sAnalyticsTrackingId: String? = null
 49→    private var sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = -1
 50→
 51→    /**
 52→     * Initialize the analytics provider - must be called prior to sending anything.
 53→     * Usage after that is static
 54→     * Note: may need to implement sampling strategy internally to limit hits, or not track Reviewer...
 55→     *
 56→     * @param context required to look up the analytics codes for the app
 57→     */
 58→    @Synchronized
 59→    fun initialize(context: Context): GoogleAnalytics? {
 60→        Timber.i("initialize()")
 61→        if (sAnalytics == null) {
 62→            Timber.d("App tracking id 'tid' = %s", getAnalyticsTag(context))
 63→            val gaConfig = GoogleAnalyticsConfig()
 64→                .setBatchingEnabled(true)
 65→                .setSamplePercentage(getAnalyticsSamplePercentage(context))
 66→                .setBatchSize(1) // until this handles application termination we will lose hits if batch>1
 67→            sAnalytics = GoogleAnalytics.builder()
 68→                .withTrackingId(getAnalyticsTag(context))
 69→                .withConfig(gaConfig)
 70→                .withDefaultRequest(
 71→                    AndroidDefaultRequest()
 72→                        .setAndroidRequestParameters(context)
 73→                        .applicationName(context.getString(R.string.app_name))
 74→                        .applicationVersion(BuildConfig.VERSION_CODE.toString())
 75→                        .applicationId(BuildConfig.APPLICATION_ID)
 76→                        .trackingId(getAnalyticsTag(context))
 77→                        .clientId(Installation.id(context))
 78→                        .anonymizeIp(context.resources.getBoolean(R.bool.ga_anonymizeIp))
 79→                )
 80→                .withHttpClient(OkHttpClientImpl(gaConfig))
 81→                .build()
 82→        }
 83→        installDefaultExceptionHandler()
 84→        val userPrefs = context.sharedPrefs()
 85→        optIn = userPrefs.getBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, false)
 86→        userPrefs.registerOnSharedPreferenceChangeListener { sharedPreferences: SharedPreferences, key: String? ->
 87→            if (key == ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY) {
 88→                val newValue = sharedPreferences.getBoolean(key, false)
 89→                Timber.i("Setting analytics opt-in to: %b", newValue)
 90→                optIn = newValue
 91→            }
 92→        }
 93→        return sAnalytics
 94→    }
 95→
 96→    private fun getAnalyticsTag(context: Context): String? {
 97→        if (sAnalyticsTrackingId == null) {
 98→            sAnalyticsTrackingId = context.getString(R.string.ga_trackingId)
 99→        }
100→        return sAnalyticsTrackingId
101→    }
102→
103→    private fun getAnalyticsSamplePercentage(context: Context): Int {
104→        if (sAnalyticsSamplePercentage == -1) {
105→            sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = context.resources.getInteger(R.integer.ga_sampleFrequency)
106→        }
107→        return sAnalyticsSamplePercentage
108→    }
109→
110→    fun setDevMode() {
111→        Timber.d("setDevMode() re-configuring for development analytics tagging")
112→        sAnalyticsTrackingId = "UA-125800786-2"
113→        sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = 100
114→        reInitialize()
115→    }
116→
117→    /**
118→     * We want to send an analytics hit on any exception, then chain to other handlers (e.g., ACRA)
119→     */
120→    @Synchronized
121→    private fun installDefaultExceptionHandler() {
122→        sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler = Thread.getDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler()
123→        Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler { thread: Thread?, throwable: Throwable ->
124→            sendAnalyticsException(throwable, true)
125→            if (thread == null) {
126→                Timber.w("unexpected: thread was null")
127→                return@setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler
128→            }
129→            sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler!!.uncaughtException(thread, throwable)
130→        }
131→    }
132→
133→    /**
134→     * Reset the default exception handler
135→     */
136→    @Synchronized
137→    private fun unInstallDefaultExceptionHandler() {
138→        Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler(sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler)
139→        sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler = null
140→    }
141→
142→    /**
143→     * Allow users to enable or disable analytics
144→     */
145→    @set:Synchronized
146→    private var optIn: Boolean
147→        get() {
148→            Timber.d("getOptIn() status: %s", sOptIn)
149→            return sOptIn
150→        }
151→        private set(optIn) {
152→            Timber.i("setOptIn(): from %s to %s", sOptIn, optIn)
153→            sOptIn = optIn
154→            sAnalytics!!.flush()
155→            sAnalytics!!.config.isEnabled = optIn
156→            sAnalytics!!.performSamplingElection()
157→            Timber.d("setOptIn() optIn / sAnalytics.config().enabled(): %s/%s", sOptIn, sAnalytics!!.config.isEnabled)
158→        }
159→
160→    /**
161→     * Set the analytics up to log things, goes to hit validator. Experimental.
162→     *
163→     * @param dryRun set to true if you want to log analytics hit but not dispatch
164→     */
165→    @Synchronized
166→    fun setDryRun(dryRun: Boolean) {
167→        Timber.i("setDryRun(): %s, warning dryRun is experimental", dryRun)
168→    }
169→
170→    /**
171→     * Re-Initialize the analytics provider
172→     */
173→    @Synchronized
174→    fun reInitialize() {
175→        // send any pending async hits, re-chain default exception handlers and re-init
176→        Timber.i("reInitialize()")
177→        sAnalytics!!.flush()
178→        sAnalytics = null
179→        unInstallDefaultExceptionHandler()
180→        initialize(AnkiDroidApp.instance.applicationContext)
181→    }
182→
183→    /**
184→     * Submit a screen for aggregation / analysis.
185→     * Intended for use to determine if / how features are being used
186→     *
187→     * @param object the result of Object.getClass().getSimpleName() will be used as the screen tag
188→     */
189→    @KotlinCleanup("rename object")
190→    fun sendAnalyticsScreenView(`object`: Any) {
191→        sendAnalyticsScreenView(`object`.javaClass.simpleName)
192→    }
193→
194→    /**
195→     * Submit a screen display with a synthetic name for aggregation / analysis
196→     * Intended for use if your class handles multiple screens you want to track separately
197→     *
198→     * @param screenName screenName the name to show in analysis reports
199→     */
200→    fun sendAnalyticsScreenView(screenName: String) {
201→        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsScreenView(): %s", screenName)
202→        if (!optIn) {
203→            return
204→        }
205→        sAnalytics!!.screenView().screenName(screenName).sendAsync()
206→    }
207→
208→    /**
209→     * Send a detailed arbitrary analytics event, with noun/verb pairs and extra data if needed
210→     *
211→     * @param category the category of event, make your own but use a constant so reporting is good
212→     * @param action   the action the user performed
213→     * @param value    A value for the event, Integer.MIN_VALUE signifies caller shouldn't send the value
214→     * @param label    A label for the event, may be null
215→     */
216→    fun sendAnalyticsEvent(category: String, action: String, value: Int? = null, label: String? = null) {
217→        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsEvent() category/action/value/label: %s/%s/%s/%s", category, action, value, label)
218→        if (!optIn) {
219→            return
220→        }
221→        val event = sAnalytics!!.event().eventCategory(category).eventAction(action)
222→        if (label != null) {
223→            event.eventLabel(label)
224→        }
225→        if (value != null) {
226→            event.eventValue(value)
227→        }
228→        event.sendAsync()
229→    }
230→
231→    /**
232→     * Send an exception event out for aggregation/analysis, parsed from the exception information
233→     *
234→     * @param t     Throwable to send for analysis
235→     * @param fatal whether it was fatal or not
236→     */
237→    fun sendAnalyticsException(t: Throwable, fatal: Boolean) {
238→        sendAnalyticsException(getCause(t).toString(), fatal)
239→    }
240→
241→    @KotlinCleanup("convert to sequence")
242→    fun getCause(t: Throwable): Throwable {
243→        var cause: Throwable?
244→        var result = t
245→        while (null != result.cause.also { cause = it } && result != cause) {
246→            result = cause!!
247→        }
248→        return result
249→    }
250→
251→    /**
252→     * Send an exception event out for aggregation/analysis
253→     *
254→     * @param description API limited to 100 characters, truncated here to 100 if needed
255→     * @param fatal       whether it was fatal or not
256→     */
257→    fun sendAnalyticsException(description: String, fatal: Boolean) {
258→        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsException() description/fatal: %s/%s", description, fatal)
259→        if (!sOptIn) {
260→            return
261→        }
262→        sAnalytics!!.exception().exceptionDescription(description).exceptionFatal(fatal).sendAsync()
263→    }
264→
265→    internal fun canGetDefaultUserAgent(): Boolean {
266→        // #5502 - getDefaultUserAgent starts a WebView. We can't have two WebViews with the same data directory.
267→        // But ACRA starts an :acra process which does not terminate when AnkiDroid is restarted. https://crbug.com/558377
268→
269→        // if we're not under the ACRA process then we're fine to initialize a WebView
270→        return if (!ACRA.isACRASenderServiceProcess()) {
271→            true
272→        } else {
273→            hasSetDataDirectory()
274→        }
275→
276→        // If we have a custom data directory, then the crash will not occur.
277→    }
278→
279→    // A listener on this preference handles the rest
280→    var isEnabled: Boolean
281→        get() {
282→            val userPrefs = AnkiDroidApp.instance.sharedPrefs()
283→            return userPrefs.getBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, false)
284→        }
285→        set(value) {
286→            // A listener on this preference handles the rest
287→            AnkiDroidApp.instance.sharedPrefs().edit {
288→                putBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, value)
289→            }
290→        }
291→
292→    @VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.NONE)
293→    fun resetForTests() {
294→        sAnalytics = null
295→    }
296→
297→    /**
298→     * An Android-specific device config generator. Without this it's "Desktop" and unknown for all hardware.
299→     * It is interesting to us what devices people use though (for instance: is Amazon Kindle support worth it?
300→     * Is anyone still using e-ink devices? How many people are on tablets? ChromeOS?)
301→     */
302→    private class AndroidDefaultRequest : DefaultRequest() {
303→        fun setAndroidRequestParameters(context: Context): DefaultRequest {
304→            // Are we running on really large screens or small screens? Send raw screen size
305→            try {
306→                val size = DisplayUtils.getDisplayDimensions(context)
307→                this.screenResolution(size.x.toString() + "x" + size.y)
308→            } catch (e: RuntimeException) {
309→                Timber.w(e)
310→                // nothing much to do here, it means we couldn't get WindowManager
311→            }
312→
313→            // We can have up to 20 of these - there might be other things we want to know
314→            // but simply seeing what hardware we are running on should be useful
315→            this.customDimension(1, Build.VERSION.RELEASE) // systemVersion, e.g. "7.1.1"  for Android 7.1.1
316→            this.customDimension(2, Build.BRAND) // brand e.g. "OnePlus"
317→            this.customDimension(3, Build.MODEL) // model e.g. "ONEPLUS A6013" for the 6T
318→            this.customDimension(4, Build.BOARD) // deviceId e.g. "sdm845" for the 6T
319→
320→            // This is important for google to auto-fingerprint us for default reporting
321→            // It is not possible to set operating system explicitly, there is no API or analytics parameter for it
322→            // Instead they respond that they auto-parse User-Agent strings for analytics attribution
323→            // For maximum analytics built-in report compatibility we will send the official WebView User-Agent string
324→            try {
325→                if (canGetDefaultUserAgent()) {
326→                    this.userAgent(WebSettings.getDefaultUserAgent(context))
327→                } else {
328→                    this.userAgent(System.getProperty("http.agent"))
329→                }
330→            } catch (e: RuntimeException) {
331→                Timber.w(e)
332→                // Catch RuntimeException as WebView initialization blows up in unpredictable ways
333→                // but analytics should never be a show-stopper
334→                this.userAgent(System.getProperty("http.agent"))
335→            }
336→            return this
337→        }
338→    }
339→
340→    object Category {
341→        const val SYNC = "Sync"
342→        const val LINK_CLICKED = "LinkClicked"
343→        const val SETTING = "Setting"
344→    }
345→
346→    /**
347→     * These Strings must not be changed as they are used for analytic comparisons between AnkiDroid versions.
348→     * If a new string is added here then the respective changes must also be made in AnalyticsConstantsTest.java
349→     * All the constant strings added here must be annotated with @AnalyticsConstant.
350→     */
351→    object Actions {
352→        /* Analytics actions used in Help Dialog*/
353→        @AnalyticsConstant
354→        val OPENED_HELPDIALOG = "Opened HelpDialogBox"
355→
356→        @AnalyticsConstant
357→        val OPENED_USING_ANKIDROID = "Opened Using AnkiDroid"
358→
359→        @AnalyticsConstant
360→        val OPENED_GET_HELP = "Opened Get Help"
361→
362→        @AnalyticsConstant

367→

370→

373→
374→        @AnalyticsConstant
375→        val OPENED_ANKIDROID_PRIVACY_POLICY = "Opened AnkiDroid Privacy Policy"
376→

378→        val OPENED_ANKIWEB_PRIVACY_POLICY = "Opened AnkiWeb Privacy Policy"
379→

 18→package com.ichi2.libanki
 19→
 20→import android.content.Context
 21→import android.media.*
 22→import android.media.AudioManager.OnAudioFocusChangeListener
 23→import android.media.MediaPlayer.OnCompletionListener
 24→import android.net.Uri
 25→import android.os.Build
 26→import androidx.annotation.CheckResult
 27→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 28→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidApp
 29→import com.ichi2.anki.ReadText
 30→import com.ichi2.compat.CompatHelper
 31→import com.ichi2.libanki.Sound.OnErrorListener.ErrorHandling.CONTINUE_AUDIO
 32→import com.ichi2.libanki.Sound.SoundSide.*
 33→import timber.log.Timber
 34→import java.util.*
 35→import java.util.regex.Pattern
 36→
 37→val VIDEO_EXTENSIONS = setOf("mp4", "mov", "mpg", "mpeg", "mkv", "avi")
 38→
 39→private typealias SoundPath = String
 40→// NICE_TO_HAVE: Abstract, then add tests for #6111
 41→/**
 42→ * Parses, loads and plays sound & video files
 43→ * Called `Sound` Anki uses `[sound:]` for both audio and video
 44→ */
 45→class Sound(private val soundPlayer: SoundPlayer, private val soundDir: String) : SoundPlayer() {
 46→    /**
 47→     * @param soundDir base path to the media files
 48→     */
 49→    constructor(soundDir: String) : this(SoundPlayer(), soundDir)
 50→
 51→    /**
 52→     * The subset of sounds to involve
 53→     * @param int Used for serialisation
 54→     */
 55→    enum class SoundSide(val int: Int) {
 56→        QUESTION(0), ANSWER(1), QUESTION_AND_ANSWER(2);
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    /** Sounds for the question/answer of a card */
 60→    // Stops code paths where QUESTION_AND_ANSWER is invalid
 61→    enum class SingleSoundSide {
 62→        QUESTION, ANSWER;
 63→
 64→        fun toSoundSide(): SoundSide = when (this) {
 65→            QUESTION -> SoundSide.QUESTION
 66→            ANSWER -> SoundSide.ANSWER
 67→        }
 68→    }
 69→
 70→    /**
 71→     * Stores sounds for the current card. Maps from a side to paths paths
 72→     * Should be accessed via [getSounds]
 73→     */
 74→    private val soundPaths: MutableMap<SoundSide, MutableList<SoundPath>> = EnumMap(SoundSide::class.java)
 75→
 76→    /** Returns a non-empty list of sounds, or null if there are no values */
 77→    @VisibleForTesting
 78→    fun getSounds(side: SoundSide) = getSoundList(side).let {
 79→        if (!it.any()) null else it
 80→    }
 81→
 82→    private fun getSoundList(side: SoundSide): List<SoundPath> {
 83→        if (side == QUESTION_AND_ANSWER) {
 84→            return getSoundList(QUESTION) + getSoundList(ANSWER)
 85→        }
 86→        return soundPaths[side] ?: emptyList()
 87→    }
 88→
 89→    /**
 90→     * Clears current sound paths; call before [expandSounds] + [addSounds]
 91→     * is called for the next card
 92→     */
 93→    fun resetSounds() {
 94→        soundPaths.clear()
 95→    }
 96→
 97→    /**
 98→     * Stores entries to the filepaths for sounds, categorized as belonging to the front (question) or back (answer) of cards.
 99→     * Note that all sounds embedded in the content will be given the same base categorization of question or answer.
100→     * Additionally, the result is to be sorted by the order of appearance on the card.
101→     * @param tags the entries expected in display order
102→     * @param side the base categorization of the sounds in the content
103→     */
104→    fun addSounds(tags: List<SoundOrVideoTag>, side: SingleSoundSide) {
105→        val soundPathCollection = soundPaths.getOrPut(side.toSoundSide()) { mutableListOf() }
106→
107→        Timber.d("Adding %d sounds to side: %s", tags.size, side)
108→        val paths = tags.map { getSoundPath(soundDir, it.filename) }
109→        soundPathCollection.addAll(paths)
110→    }
111→
112→    /** Plays all the sounds for the indicated side(s)  */
113→    fun playSounds(side: SoundSide, errorListener: OnErrorListener?) {
114→        // If there are sounds to play for the current card, start with the first one
115→        val soundPaths = getSounds(side) ?: return
116→        Timber.d("playSounds: playing $side")
117→        this.playSound(
118→            soundPaths[0],
119→            PlayAllCompletionListener(side, errorListener),
120→            errorListener
121→        )
122→    }
123→
124→    /** Returns the total length of all sounds for the side in milliseconds */
125→    fun getSoundsLength(side: SoundSide): Long {
126→        val soundPaths = getSounds(side) ?: return 0
127→        val metaRetriever = MediaMetadataRetriever()
128→        val context = AnkiDroidApp.instance.applicationContext
129→        return soundPaths
130→            .map { Uri.parse(it) }
131→            .sumOf { uri ->
132→                try {
133→                    metaRetriever.getDuration(context, uri)
134→                } catch (e: Exception) {
135→                    Timber.w(
136→                        e,
137→                        "metaRetriever - Error setting Data Source for mediaRetriever (media doesn't exist or forbidden?)."
138→                    )
139→                    0
140→                }
141→            }
142→    }
143→
144→    /**
145→     * Class used to play all sounds for a given card side
146→     */
147→    private inner class PlayAllCompletionListener(
148→        private val side: SoundSide,
149→        private val errorListener: OnErrorListener?
150→    ) : OnCompletionListener {
151→        /** Index of next sound to play inside `getSounds(side)` */
152→        // this is a completion listener: [onCompletion] is first called after the first sound
153→        private var nextIndexToPlay = 1
154→        override fun onCompletion(mp: MediaPlayer) {
155→            val paths = getSounds(side) ?: emptyList() // emptyList -> stopSounds()
156→            // If there are still more sounds to play for the current card, play the next one
157→            if (nextIndexToPlay < paths.size) {
158→                Timber.i("Play all: Playing next sound")
159→                playSound(paths[nextIndexToPlay++], this, errorListener)
160→            } else {
161→                Timber.i("Play all: Completed - releasing sound")
162→                soundPlayer.stopSounds()
163→            }
164→        }
165→    }
166→
167→    override fun stopSounds() {
168→        soundPlayer.stopSounds()
169→        ReadText.stopTts() // TODO: Reconsider design
170→    }
171→
172→    fun hasQuestion(): Boolean = getSounds(QUESTION) != null
173→
174→    fun hasAnswer(): Boolean = getSounds(ANSWER) != null
175→
176→    fun interface OnErrorListener {
177→        fun onError(mp: MediaPlayer?, which: Int, extra: Int, path: String?): ErrorHandling
178→
179→        enum class ErrorHandling {
180→            /** Stop playing audio */
181→            STOP_AUDIO,
182→
183→            /** Continue to the next audio (if any) */
184→            CONTINUE_AUDIO,
185→
186→            /** Retry the current audio */
187→            RETRY_AUDIO
188→        }
189→    }
190→
191→    companion object {
192→        /**
193→         * Pattern used to identify the markers for sound files
194→         */
195→        val SOUND_PATTERN: Pattern = Pattern.compile("\\[sound:([^\\[\\]]*)]")
196→
197→        // TODO join with SOUND_PATTERN
198→        val SOUND_RE = SOUND_PATTERN.toRegex()
199→
200→        /**
201→         * Pattern used to parse URI (according to http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3986#page-50)
202→         */
203→        private val sUriPattern =
204→            Pattern.compile("^(([^:/?#]+):)?(//([^/?#]*))?([^?#]*)(\\?([^#]*))?(#(.*))?$")
205→
206→        /** Extract SoundOrVideoTag instances from content where sound tags are in the form: [sound:filename.mp3]  */
207→        @CheckResult
208→        fun extractTagsFromLegacyContent(content: String): List<SoundOrVideoTag> {
209→            val matcher = SOUND_PATTERN.matcher(content)
210→            // While there is matches of the pattern for sound markers
211→            val ret = mutableListOf<SoundOrVideoTag>()
212→            while (matcher.find()) {
213→                // Get the sound file name
214→                val sound = matcher.group(1)!!
215→                ret.add(SoundOrVideoTag(sound))
216→            }
217→            return ret
218→        }
219→
220→        /**
221→         * expandSounds takes content with embedded sound file placeholders and expands them to reference the actual media
222→         * file
223→         *
224→         * @param content -- card content to be rendered that may contain embedded audio
225→         * @return -- the same content but in a format that will render working play buttons when audio was embedded
226→         */
227→        fun expandSounds(content: String): String {
228→            return addPlayIcons(content)
229→        }
230→
231→        /**
232→         * @param soundDir -- base path to the media files.
233→         * @param sound -- path to the sound file from the card content.
234→         * @return absolute URI to the sound file.
235→         */
236→        fun getSoundPath(soundDir: String, sound: String): String {
237→            val trimmedSound = sound.trim { it <= ' ' }
238→            return if (hasURIScheme(trimmedSound)) {
239→                trimmedSound
240→            } else {
241→                soundDir + Uri.encode(sound.trimEnd())
242→            }
243→        }
244→
245→        /**
246→         * @param path -- path to the sound file from the card content.
247→         * @return true if path is well-formed URI and contains URI scheme.
248→         */
249→        private fun hasURIScheme(path: String): Boolean {
250→            val uriMatcher = sUriPattern.matcher(path.trim { it <= ' ' })
251→            return uriMatcher.matches() && uriMatcher.group(2) != null
252→        }
253→    }
254→}
255→
256→open class SoundPlayer {
257→    val currentAudioUri: String?
258→        get() = mCurrentAudioUri?.toString()
259→
260→    val isCurrentAudioFinished: Boolean
261→        get() = mMediaPlayer == null
262→
263→    /**
264→     * Media player used to play the sounds. It's Nullable and that it is set only if a sound is playing or paused, otherwise it is null.
265→     */
266→    private var mMediaPlayer: MediaPlayer? = null
267→
268→    private var mCurrentAudioUri: Uri? = null
269→
270→    /**
271→     * AudioManager to request/release audio focus
272→     */
273→    private var mAudioManager: AudioManager? = null
274→
275→    private var mAudioFocusRequest: AudioFocusRequest? = null
276→
277→    /**
278→     * Plays the given sound or video and sets playAllListener if available on media player to start next media.
279→     * If videoView is null and the media is a video, then a request is sent to start the VideoPlayer Activity
280→     */
281→    fun playSound(
282→        soundPath: String,
283→        onCompletionListener: OnCompletionListener?,
284→        errorListener: Sound.OnErrorListener?
285→    ) {
286→        Timber.d("Playing single sound")
287→        val completionListener = onCompletionListener ?: SingleSoundCompletionListener()
288→        val errorHandler = errorListener
289→            ?: Sound.OnErrorListener { _: MediaPlayer?, what: Int, extra: Int, _: String? ->
290→                Timber.w("Media Error: (%d, %d). Calling OnCompletionListener", what, extra)
291→                CONTINUE_AUDIO
292→            }
293→        playSoundInternal(soundPath, completionListener, errorHandler)
294→    }
295→
296→    /**
297→     * Plays a sound without ensuring that the playAllListener will release the audio
298→     */
299→    private fun playSoundInternal(
300→        soundPath: String,
301→        completionListener: OnCompletionListener,
302→        errorHandler: Sound.OnErrorListener
303→    ) {
304→        Timber.d("Playing %s", soundPath)
305→        val soundUri = Uri.parse(soundPath)
306→        mCurrentAudioUri = soundUri
307→
308→        val context = AnkiDroidApp.instance.applicationContext
309→
310→        // Play media
311→        fun playMedia() {
312→            try {
313→                // Create media player
314→                if (mMediaPlayer == null) {
315→                    Timber.d("Creating media player for playback")
316→                    mMediaPlayer = MediaPlayer()
317→                } else {
318→                    Timber.d("Resetting media for playback")
319→                    mMediaPlayer!!.reset()
320→                }
321→                val mediaPlayer = mMediaPlayer!!
322→                mAudioManager =
323→                    mAudioManager ?: context.getSystemService(Context.AUDIO_SERVICE) as AudioManager
324→
325→                mediaPlayer.setOnErrorListener { mp: MediaPlayer?, which: Int, extra: Int ->
326→                    val errorHandling = errorHandler.onError(
327→                        mp,
328→                        which,
329→                        extra,
330→                        soundPath
331→                    )
332→                    // returning false calls onComplete()
333→                    return@setOnErrorListener when (errorHandling) {
334→                        CONTINUE_AUDIO -> false
335→                        Sound.OnErrorListener.ErrorHandling.RETRY_AUDIO -> {
336→                            playMedia()
337→                            true
338→                        }
339→                        Sound.OnErrorListener.ErrorHandling.STOP_AUDIO -> {
340→                            stopSounds()
341→                            true
342→                        }
343→                    }
344→                }
345→                // Setup the MediaPlayer
346→                mediaPlayer.setDataSource(context, soundUri)
347→                mediaPlayer.setAudioAttributes(
348→                    AudioAttributes.Builder()
349→                        .setContentType(AudioAttributes.CONTENT_TYPE_MUSIC)
350→                        .build()
351→                )
352→                mediaPlayer.setOnPreparedListener {
353→                    Timber.d("Starting media player")
354→                    it.start()
355→                }
356→                mediaPlayer.setOnCompletionListener(completionListener)
357→                mediaPlayer.prepareAsync()
358→                Timber.d("Requesting audio focus")
359→
360→                // Set mAudioFocusRequest for API 26 and above.
361→                if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
362→                    mAudioFocusRequest =
363→                        AudioFocusRequest.Builder(AudioManager.AUDIOFOCUS_GAIN_TRANSIENT_MAY_DUCK)
364→                            .setOnAudioFocusChangeListener(audioFocusChangeListener)
365→                            .build()
366→                }
367→                CompatHelper.compat.requestAudioFocus(
368→                    mAudioManager!!,
369→                    audioFocusChangeListener,
370→                    mAudioFocusRequest
371→                )
372→            } catch (e: Exception) {
373→                Timber.e(e, "playSounds - Error reproducing sound %s", soundPath)
374→                when (
375→                    errorHandler.onError(
376→                        mMediaPlayer,
377→                        MediaPlayer.MEDIA_ERROR_UNSUPPORTED,
378→                        0,
379→                        soundPath
380→                    )
381→                ) {
382→                    CONTINUE_AUDIO -> {
383→                        Timber.d("Force playing next sound.")
384→                        completionListener.onCompletion(mMediaPlayer)
385→                    }
386→                    Sound.OnErrorListener.ErrorHandling.STOP_AUDIO -> stopSounds()
387→                    Sound.OnErrorListener.ErrorHandling.RETRY_AUDIO -> playMedia()
388→                }
389→            }
390→        }
391→
392→        playMedia()
393→    }
394→
395→    /**
396→     * Releases the sound.
397→     */
398→    private fun releaseSound() {
399→        Timber.d("Releasing sounds and abandoning audio focus")
400→        mMediaPlayer?.let {
401→            // Required to remove warning: "mediaplayer went away with unhandled events"
402→            // https://stackoverflow.com/questions/9609479/android-mediaplayer-went-away-with-unhandled-events
403→            it.reset()
404→            it.release()
405→            mMediaPlayer = null
406→        }
407→        mAudioManager?.let {
408→            // mAudioFocusRequest was initialised for API 26 and above in playSoundInternal().
409→            CompatHelper.compat.abandonAudioFocus(it, audioFocusChangeListener, mAudioFocusRequest)
410→            mAudioManager = null
411→        }
412→    }
413→
414→    open fun stopSounds() {
415→        mMediaPlayer?.let {
416→            it.stop()
417→            // TODO: Inefficient. Determine whether we want to release or stop, don't do both
418→            // Ensure `currentAudioUri` etc... still work when we do this
419→            releaseSound()
420→        }
421→    }
422→
423→    fun playOrPauseSound() {
424→        mMediaPlayer?.let {
425→            if (it.isPlaying) {
426→                it.pause()
427→            } else {
428→                it.start()
429→            }
430→        }
431→    }
432→
433→    /** #5414 - Ensures playing a single sound performs cleanup  */
434→    private inner class SingleSoundCompletionListener : OnCompletionListener {
435→        override fun onCompletion(mp: MediaPlayer) {
436→            Timber.d("Single Sound completed")
437→            releaseSound()
438→        }
439→    }
440→
441→    companion object {
442→        /**
443→         * Listener to handle audio focus. Currently blank because we're not respecting losing focus from other apps.
444→         */
445→        private val audioFocusChangeListener = OnAudioFocusChangeListener { }
446→    }
447→}
448→
449→@Throws(Exception::class)
450→private fun MediaMetadataRetriever.getDuration(context: Context, uri: Uri): Long {
451→    this.setDataSource(context, uri)
452→    val duration = this.extractMetadata(MediaMetadataRetriever.METADATA_KEY_DURATION)
453→    return duration!!.toLong()
454→}

content.text:
Now I understand the issue. The preference key is already defined in the XML files, but there's no actual implementation to respect the preference. The preference is in `preferences_appearance.xml` but:

1. There's no string value for "Show play buttons on cards with audio" (the `show_audio_play_buttons_key` is defined in preferences.xml but no corresponding strings exist in 10-preferences.xml)
2. The `expandSounds` method in `Sound.kt` always calls `addPlayIcons` without checking the preference
3. The analytics tracking doesn't include this preference

Let me implement the fix:

function.arguments: {"file_path":"/tmp/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md","content":"# Implementation Plan: Show play buttons on cards with audio preference ## Overview Add a preference to AnkiDroid matching Anki Desktop's "Show play buttons on cards with audio" feature. This allows users to hide/show play buttons on cards containing audio. ## Files to Modify ### 1. `/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/10-preferences.xml` **Add after line 184 (after ``):** ```xml Show play buttons on cards with audio Show or hide play buttons on the card content ``` ### 2. `/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/res/xml/preferences_appearance.xml` **Update lines 114-117, change from:** ```xml ``` **To:** ```xml ``` ### 3. `/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/SoundKt.kt` **Update the `addPlayIcons` function (lines 63-74):** ```kotlin fun addPlayIcons(content: String, showIcons: Boolean = true): String { if (!showIcons) return content return AV_REF_RE.replace(content) { match -> val groups = match.groupValues val side = groups[2] val index = groups[3] val playsound = "playsound:$side:$index" """ """ } } ``` ### 4. `/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/Sound.kt` **Update the `expandSounds` companion method (line 227-229):** ```kotlin fun expandSounds(content: String): String { return addPlayIcons(content) } ``` **To:** ```kotlin fun expandSounds(content: String, showIcons: Boolean = true): String { return addPlayIcons(content, showIcons) } ``` ### 5. `/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/HtmlGenerator.kt` **Add context parameter to `expandSounds` method:** First, update the method signature (line 51-53): ```kotlin fun expandSounds(context: Context, content: String): String { return Sound.expandSounds(context, content) } ``` Actually, looking at how this is structured, we need to check how preferences are accessed. Let me look at the CardAppearance class instead since preferences are stored there. ### 6. `/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/CardAppearance.kt` **Add preference check. First let me see what this file contains:** - Need to read the file first to understand its structure ### 7. `/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/preferences/AppearanceSettingsFragment.kt` **Add preference handler (around line 143, after show_progress_preference handler):** ```kotlin // Show play buttons on cards with audio requirePreference(R.string.show_audio_play_buttons_key).apply { launchCatchingTask { isChecked = context.sharedPrefs().getBoolean("showAudioPlayButtons", true) } setOnPreferenceChangeListener { _, newValue -> val newCheckedValue = newValue as? Boolean ?: return@setOnPreferenceChangeListener false context.sharedPrefs().edit { putBoolean("showAudioPlayButtons", newCheckedValue) } true } } ``` ### 8. `/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/analytics/UsageAnalytics.kt` **Add "showAudioPlayButtons" to `preferencesWhoseChangesShouldBeReported` set (around line 489):** ```kotlin "showAudioPlayButtons", // Show play buttons on cards with audio ``` ## Implementation Details The key insight is that the `expandSounds` method always calls `addPlayIcons` without checking a preference. We need to: 1. Add a preference to store the user's choice (Android shared preferences) 2. Read that preference when rendering cards 3. Pass it to `expandSounds` which passes it to `addPlayIcons` 4. If the preference says "don't show", `addPlayIcons` returns the content unchanged (no play buttons added) ## Notes - The preference key in preferences.xml is `show_audio_play_buttons_key` which maps to the value "showAudioPlayButtons" in preferences.xml - Default should be true (show play buttons) to maintain backward compatibility - Anki Desktop stores this in collection config, but for AnkiDroid we use app preferences since it's UI-level preference"}

124→        return when (requiresMathjax) {
125→            false -> ""
126→            true ->
127→                """        <script src="/assets/mathjax/conf.js"> </script>
128→        <script src="/assets/mathjax/tex-chtml.js"> </script>"""
129→        }
130→    }
131→
132→    companion object {
133→        fun createInstance(card: Card, reload: Boolean, side: Side, context: HtmlGenerator): CardHtml {
134→            val content = displayString(card, reload, side, context)
135→
136→            val nightModeInversion = currentTheme.isNightMode && !hasUserDefinedNightMode(card)
137→
138→            val renderOutput = card.renderOutput()
139→            val questionAv = renderOutput.question_av_tags
140→            val answerAv = renderOutput.answer_av_tags
141→            val questionSound: List<SoundOrVideoTag> =
142→                questionAv.filterIsInstance(SoundOrVideoTag::class.java)
143→            val answerSound: List<SoundOrVideoTag> = answerAv.filterIsInstance(SoundOrVideoTag::class.java)
144→
145→            // legacy (slow) function to return the answer without the front side
146→            fun getAnswerWithoutFrontSideLegacy(): String = removeFrontSideAudio(card, card.a())
147→
148→            return CardHtml(content, card.ord, nightModeInversion, context, side, ::getAnswerWithoutFrontSideLegacy, questionSound, answerSound)
149→        }

1→/*

 14→ *  this program.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.
 15→ */
 16→
 17→package com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer
 18→
 19→import android.content.Context
 20→import android.content.res.Resources
 21→import androidx.annotation.CheckResult
 22→import com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 23→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.ReviewerCustomFonts
 24→import com.ichi2.libanki.Card
 25→import com.ichi2.libanki.Sound
 26→import timber.log.Timber
 27→import java.io.BufferedReader
 28→import java.io.IOException
 29→import java.io.InputStream
 30→import java.io.InputStreamReader
 31→
 32→class HtmlGenerator(
 33→    private val typeAnswer: TypeAnswer,
 34→    val cardAppearance: CardAppearance,
 35→    val cardTemplate: CardTemplate,
 36→    val resources: Resources
 37→) {
 38→
 39→    @CheckResult
 40→    fun generateHtml(card: Card, reload: Boolean, side: Side): CardHtml {
 41→        return CardHtml.createInstance(card, reload, side, this)
 42→    }
 43→
 44→    fun filterTypeAnswer(content: String, side: Side): String {
 45→        return when (side) {
 46→            Side.FRONT -> typeAnswer.filterQuestion(content)
 47→            Side.BACK -> typeAnswer.filterAnswer(content)
 48→        }
 49→    }
 50→
 51→    fun expandSounds(content: String): String {
 52→        return Sound.expandSounds(content)
 53→    }
 54→
 55→    companion object {
 56→        fun createInstance(
 57→            context: Context,
 58→            typeAnswer: TypeAnswer
 59→        ): HtmlGenerator {
 60→            val preferences = context.sharedPrefs()
 61→            val cardAppearance = CardAppearance.create(ReviewerCustomFonts(), preferences)
 62→            val cardHtmlTemplate = loadCardTemplate(context)
 63→
 64→            return HtmlGenerator(
 65→                typeAnswer,
 66→                cardAppearance,
 67→                cardHtmlTemplate,
 68→                context.resources
 69→            )
 70→        }
 71→
 72→        /**
 73→         * Load the template for the card
 74→         */
 75→        private fun loadCardTemplate(viewer: Context): CardTemplate {
 76→            try {
 77→                val data = convertStreamToString(viewer.assets.open("card_template.html"))
 78→                return CardTemplate(data)
 79→            } catch (e: IOException) {
 80→                Timber.w(e)
 81→                throw RuntimeException(e)
 82→            }
 83→        }
 84→
 85→        /**
 86→         * Converts an InputStream to a String.
 87→         *
 88→         * @param input InputStream to convert
 89→         * @return String version of the InputStream
 90→         */
 91→        private fun convertStreamToString(input: InputStream?): String {
 92→            var contentOfMyInputStream = ""
 93→            try {
 94→                val rd = BufferedReader(InputStreamReader(input), 4096)
 95→                var line: String?
 96→                val sb = StringBuilder()
 97→                while (rd.readLine().also { line = it } != null) {
 98→                    sb.append(line)
 99→                }
100→                rd.close()
101→                contentOfMyInputStream = sb.toString()
102→            } catch (e: Exception) {
103→                Timber.w(e)
104→            }
105→            return contentOfMyInputStream
106→        }
107→    }
108→}
109→

play buttons to the content **Objective:** Make this flow respect the "showAudioPlayButtons" preference. ## Implementation Steps ### Step 1: Add String Resources **File:** `/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/10-preferences.xml` **After line 184 (after `Show play buttons on cards with audio Show or hide play buttons on the card content ``` ### Step 2: Update Preference XML **File:** `/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/res/xml/preferences_appearance.xml` **Lines 114-117, change from:** ```xml ``` **To:** ```xml ``` ### Step 3: Update SoundKt.kt **File:** `/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/SoundKt.kt` **Update `addPlayIcons` function (lines 63-74):** ```kotlin fun addPlayIcons(content: String, showIcons: Boolean = true): String { if (!showIcons) return content return AV_REF_RE.replace(content) { match -> val groups = match.groupValues val side = groups[2] val index = groups[3] val playsound = "playsound:$side:$index" """ """ } } ``` ### Step 4: Update Sound.kt **File:** `/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/Sound.kt` **Update `expandSounds` companion method (lines 227-229):** ```kotlin fun expandSounds(content: String, showIcons: Boolean = true): String { return addPlayIcons(content, showIcons) } ``` ### Step 5: Update CardAppearance.kt **File:** `/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/CardAppearance.kt` **Add a property to store the preference (after line 26):** ```kotlin class CardAppearance( private val customFonts: ReviewerCustomFonts, private val cardZoom: Int, private val imageZoom: Int, private val centerVertically: Boolean, private val showAudioPlayButtons: Boolean = true ) { ``` **Update the `create` method (lines 78-83):** ```kotlin fun create(customFonts: ReviewerCustomFonts, preferences: SharedPreferences): CardAppearance { val cardZoom = preferences.getInt("cardZoom", 100) val imageZoom = preferences.getInt("imageZoom", 100) val centerVertically = preferences.getBoolean("centerVertically", false) val showAudioPlayButtons = preferences.getBoolean("showAudioPlayButtons", true) return CardAppearance(customFonts, cardZoom, imageZoom, centerVertically, showAudioPlayButtons) } ``` ### Step 6: Update HtmlGenerator.kt **File:** `/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/HtmlGenerator.kt` **Update `expandSounds` method (lines 51-53):** ```kotlin fun expandSounds(content: String): String { return Sound.expandSounds(content, cardAppearance.showAudioPlayButtons) } ``` **Note:** `cardAppearance` is already a property of `HtmlGenerator`, so we can access it directly. ### Step 7: Update AppearanceSettingsFragment.kt **File:** `/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/preferences/AppearanceSettingsFragment.kt` **Add preference handler (after line 143, after `show_progress_preference` handler):** ```kotlin // Show play buttons requirePreference(R.string.show_audio_play_buttons_key).apply { launchCatchingTask { isChecked = context.sharedPrefs().getBoolean("showAudioPlayButtons", true) } setOnPreferenceChangeListener { _, newValue -> val newCheckedValue = newValue as? Boolean ?: return@setOnPreferenceChangeListener false context.sharedPrefs().edit { putBoolean("showAudioPlayButtons", newCheckedValue) } true } } ``` ### Step 8: Update Analysis and Analytics **File:** `/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/analytics/UsageAnalytics.kt` **Add to `preferencesWhoseChangesShouldBeReported` set (around line 489, after "showETA"):** ```kotlin "showAudioPlayButtons", // Show play buttons on cards with audio ``` ## Testing Checklist 1. Preference appears in Appearance settings 2. Toggling preference off/on works 3. Cards with audio show/hide play buttons based on preference 4. Preference persists across app restarts 5. Default value is `true` (play buttons shown) 6. Analytics correctly track preference changes ## Notes - Default value `true` maintains backward compatibility - Preference is stored in Android shared preferences (not collection config) because it's UI-level setting - The `android:defaultValue="true"` in XML and `getBoolean("showAudioPlayButtons", true)` in code ensure consistency - Analytics tracking helps understand feature adoption"}

"""
Script to apply all changes for the "Show play buttons on cards with audio" feature

import os
import re

def update_file(file_path, replacements):
    """Update a file by applying a list of (pattern, replacement) tuples"""
    with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
        content = f.read()

    for pattern, replacement in replacements:
        content = re.sub(pattern, replacement, content, flags=re.MULTILINE | re.DOTALL)

    with open(file_path, 'w') as f:
        f.write(content)

    print(f"Updated: {file_path}")

def main():
    base_path = "/project/AnkiDroid"

    # 1. Update 10-preferences.xml - Add string resources
    file1 = f"{base_path}/src/main/res/values/10-preferences.xml"
    update_file(file1, [
        (r'(<string name="accessibility" maxLength="41">Accessibility</string>\n    )(<!-- Paste clipboard image as png option -->)',
         r'\1<string name="show_audio_play_buttons" maxLength="41">Show play buttons on cards with audio</string>\n    <string name="show_audio_play_buttons_summ">Show or hide play buttons on the card content</string>\n    \2'),
    ])

    # 2. Update preferences_appearance.xml - Add proper title/summary
    file2 = f"{base_path}/src/main/res/xml/preferences_appearance.xml"
    update_file(file2, [
        (r'(<SwitchPreferenceCompat\n        android:key="@string/show_audio_play_buttons_key"\n        android:defaultValue="true"\n        tools:title="Show play buttons on cards with audio"\n        />)',
         r'<SwitchPreferenceCompat\n        android:key="@string/show_audio_play_buttons_key"\n        android:title="@string/show_audio_play_buttons"\n        android:summary="@string/show_audio_play_buttons_summ"\n        android:defaultValue="true"\n        />'),
    ])

    # 3. Update SoundKt.kt - Add showIcons parameter to addPlayIcons
    file3 = f"{base_path}/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/SoundKt.kt"
    update_file(file3, [
        (r'(fun stripAvRefs\(text: String\) = AV_REF_RE\.replace\("", text\)\s*\n)(fun addPlayIcons\(content: String\): String \{)',
         r'\1fun addPlayIcons(content: String, showIcons: Boolean = true): String {\n    if (!showIcons) return content\n    '),
    ])

    # 4. Update Sound.kt - Add showIcons parameter to expandSounds
    file4 = f"{base_path}/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/Sound.kt"
    update_file(file4, [
        (r'(fun expandSounds\(content: String\): String \{\s*)(return addPlayIcons\(content\)\s})',
         r'\1return addPlayIcons(content, showIcons)\n}'),
    ])

    # 5. Update CardAppearance.kt - Add showAudioPlayButtons property
    file5 = f"{base_path}/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/CardAppearance.kt"
    update_file(file5, [
        (r'(class CardAppearance\(private val customFonts: ReviewerCustomFonts, private val cardZoom: Int, private val imageZoom: Int, private val centerVertically: Boolean\) \{)',
         r'class CardAppearance(\n    private val customFonts: ReviewerCustomFonts,\n    private val cardZoom: Int,\n    private val imageZoom: Int,\n    private val centerVertically: Boolean,\n    private val showAudioPlayButtons: Boolean = true\n) {'),
        (r'(fun create\(customFonts: ReviewerCustomFonts, preferences: SharedPreferences\): CardAppearance \{\s*\n)(val cardZoom = preferences\.getInt\("cardZoom", 100\))',
         r'\1\2\n        val imageZoom = preferences.getInt("imageZoom", 100)\n        val centerVertically = preferences.getBoolean("centerVertically", false)\n        val showAudioPlayButtons = preferences.getBoolean("showAudioPlayButtons", true)'),
        (r'(return CardAppearance\(customFonts, cardZoom, imageZoom, centerVertically\)\s*\n})',
         r'return CardAppearance(customFonts, cardZoom, imageZoom, centerVertically, showAudioPlayButtons)\n    }'),
    ])

    # 6. Update HtmlGenerator.kt - Update expandSounds to use preference
    file6 = f"{base_path}/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/HtmlGenerator.kt"
    update_file(file6, [

Boolean ?: return@setOnPreferenceChangeListener false\n                context.sharedPrefs().edit {\n                    putBoolean("showAudioPlayButtons", newCheckedValue)\n                }\n                true\n            }\n        }\n\1'),

{"file_path":"/tmp/apply_single_changes.py","content":"#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Apply changes one file at a time for the "Show play buttons on cards with audio" feature
"""

import os

def apply_file_changes(filepath, old_text, new_text):
    """Apply a single change to a file"""
    with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
        content = f.read()

    if old_text in content:
        content = content.replace(old_text, new_text)
        with open(filepath, 'w') as f:
            f.write(content)
        print(f"✓ Updated: {filepath}")
        return True
    else:
        print(f"✗ Pattern not found in: {filepath}")
        print(f"Looking for: {old_text[:100]}")
        return False

base_path = "/project/AnkiDroid"

# 1. Update 10-preferences.xml
file1 = f"{base_path}/src/main/res/values/10-preferences.xml"
old1 = '<string name="show_deck_title" maxLength="41">Show deck title</string>\n    <string name="accessibility" maxLength="41">Accessibility</string>\n    <!-- Paste clipboard image as png option -->'
new1 = '<string name="show_deck_title" maxLength="41">Show deck title</string>\n    <string name="accessibility" maxLength="41">Accessibility</string>\n    <string name="show_audio_play_buttons" maxLength="41">Show play buttons on cards with audio</string>\n    <string name="show_audio_play_buttons_summ">Show or hide play buttons on the card content</string>\n    <!-- Paste clipboard image as png option -->'
success1 = apply_file_changes(file1, old1, new1)

# 2. Update preferences_appearance.xml
file2 = f"{base_path}/src/main/res/xml/preferences_appearance.xml"
old2 = '''<SwitchPreferenceCompat
            android:key="@string/show_audio_play_buttons_key"
            android:defaultValue="true"
            tools:title="Show play buttons on cards with audio"
            />'''
new2 = '''<SwitchPreferenceCompat
            android:key="@string/show_audio_play_buttons_key"
            android:title="@string/show_audio_play_buttons"
            android:summary="@string/show_audio_play_buttons_summ"
            android:defaultValue="true"
            />'''
success2 = apply_file_changes(file2, old2, new2)

# 3. Update SoundKt.kt
file3 = f"{base_path}/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/SoundKt.kt"
old3 = 'fun stripAvRefs(text: String) = AV_REF_RE.replace("", text)\n\nfun addPlayIcons(content: String): String {'
new3 = 'fun stripAvRefs(text: String) = AV_REF_RE.replace("", text)\n\nfun addPlayIcons(content: String, showIcons: Boolean = true): String {\n    if (!showIcons) return content'
success3 = apply_file_changes(file3, old3, new3)

# 4. Update Sound.kt
file4 = f"{base_path}/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/Sound.kt"
old4 = 'fun expandSounds(content: String): String {\n            return addPlayIcons(content)\n        }'
new4 = 'fun expandSounds(content: String, showIcons: Boolean = true): String {\n            return addPlayIcons(content, showIcons)\n        }'
success4 = apply_file_changes(file4, old4, new4)

# 5. Update CardAppearance.kt (constructor)
file5a = f"{base_path}/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/CardAppearance.kt"
old5a = 'class CardAppearance(private val customFonts: ReviewerCustomFonts, private val cardZoom: Int, private val imageZoom: Int, private val centerVertically: Boolean) {'
new5a = 'class CardAppearance(\n    private val customFonts: ReviewerCustomFonts,\n    private val cardZoom: Int,\n    private val imageZoom: Int,\n    private val centerVertically: Boolean,\n    private val showAudioPlayButtons: Boolean = true\n) {'
success5a = apply_file_changes(file5a, old5a, new5a)

# 5b. Update CardAppearance.create() method
file5b = file5a
old5b = 'fun create(customFonts: ReviewerCustomFonts, preferences: SharedPreferences): CardAppearance {\n            val cardZoom = preferences.getInt("cardZoom", 100)\n            val imageZoom = preferences.getInt("imageZoom", 100)\n            val centerVertically = preferences.getBoolean("centerVertically", false)\n            return CardAppearance(customFonts, cardZoom, imageZoom, centerVertically)\n        }'
new5b = 'fun create(customFonts: ReviewerCustomFonts, preferences: SharedPreferences): CardAppearance {\n            val cardZoom = preferences.getInt("cardZoom", 100)\n            val imageZoom = preferences.getInt("imageZoom", 100)\n            val centerVertically = preferences.getBoolean("centerVertically", false)\n            val showAudioPlayButtons = preferences.getBoolean("showAudioPlayButtons", true)\n            return CardAppearance(customFonts, cardZoom, imageZoom, centerVertically, showAudioPlayButtons)\n        }'
success5b = apply_file_changes(file5b, old5b, new5b)

# 6. Update HtmlGenerator.kt
file6 = f"{base_path}/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/HtmlGenerator.kt"
old6 = 'fun expandSounds(content: String): String {\n        return Sound.expandSounds(content)\n    }'
new6 = 'fun expandSounds(content: String): String {\n        return Sound.expandSounds(content, cardAppearance.showAudioPlayButtons)\n    }'
success6 = apply_file_changes(file6, old6, new6)

# 7. Update AppearanceSettingsFragment.kt
file7 = f"{base_path}/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/preferences/AppearanceSettingsFragment.kt"
old7 = '''// Represents the collection pref "dueCounts": i.e.
        // whether the remaining number of cards should be shown.
        requirePreference<SwitchPreferenceCompat>(R.string.show_progress_preference).apply {
            launchCatchingTask { isChecked = withCol { config.get("dueCounts") ?: true } }
            setOnPreferenceChangeListener { _, newDueCountsValue ->
                val newDueCountsValueBool = newDueCountsValue as? Boolean ?: return@setOnPreferenceChangeListener false
                launchCatchingTask { withCol { config.set("dueCounts", newDueCountsValueBool) } }
                true
            }
        }'''
new7 = '''// Represents the collection pref "dueCounts": i.e.
        // whether the remaining number of cards should be shown.
        requirePreference<SwitchPreferenceCompat>(R.string.show_progress_preference).apply {
            launchCatchingTask { isChecked = withCol { config.get("dueCounts") ?: true } }
            setOnPreferenceChangeListener { _, newDueCountsValue ->
                val newDueCountsValueBool = newDueCountsValue as? Boolean ?: return@setOnPreferenceChangeListener false
                launchCatchingTask { withCol { config.set("dueCounts", newDueCountsValueBool) } }
                true
            }
        }
        // Show play buttons on cards with audio
        requirePreference<SwitchPreferenceCompat>(R.string.show_audio_play_buttons_key).apply {
            launchCatchingTask { isChecked = context.sharedPrefs().getBoolean("showAudioPlayButtons", true) }
            setOnPreferenceChangeListener { _, newValue ->
                val newCheckedValue = newValue as? Boolean ?: return@setOnPreferenceChangeListener false
                context.sharedPrefs().edit {
                    putBoolean("showAudioPlayButtons", newCheckedValue)
                }
                true
            }
        }'''
success7 = apply_file_changes(file7, old7, new7)

# 8. Update UsageAnalytics.kt
file8 = f"{base_path}/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/analytics/UsageAnalytics.kt"
old8 = '''"showETA", // Show ETA
        // Controls'''
new8 = '''"showETA", // Show ETA
        "showAudioPlayButtons", // Show play buttons on cards with audio
        // Controls'''
success8 = apply_file_changes(file8, old8, new8)

print("\n" + "="*60)
print("SUMMARY:")
print(f"10-preferences.xml: {success1}")
print(f"preferences_appearance.xml: {success2}")
print(f"SoundKt.kt: {success3}")
print(f"Sound.kt: {success4}")
print(f"CardAppearance.kt (constructor): {success5a}")
print(f"CardAppearance.kt (create): {success5b}")
print(f"HtmlGenerator.kt: {success6}")
print(f"AppearanceSettingsFragment.kt: {success7}")
print(f"UsageAnalytics.kt: {success8}")
print("="*60)"}

{"command":"cd /project && python3 << 'EOF'
with open('AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/analytics/UsageAnalytics.kt', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

old = '''"showETA", // Show ETA
        "card_browser_show_media_filenames", // Display filenames in card browser'''

new = '''"showETA", // Show ETA
        "card_browser_show_media_filenames", // Display filenames in card browser
        "showAudioPlayButtons", // Show play buttons on cards with audio'''

if old in content:
    content = content.replace(old, new)
    with open('AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/analytics/UsageAnalytics.kt', 'w') as f:
        f.write(content)
    print("✓ Updated UsageAnalytics.kt")
else:
    print("Pattern still not found")
EOF","explanation":"Update UsageAnalytics.kt with correct pattern"}

{"file_path":"/tmp/IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md","content":"# Implementation Summary: Show play buttons on cards with audio preference

## Overview
Successfully implemented a preference setting in AnkiDroid to match Anki Desktop's "Show play buttons on cards with audio" feature. This allows users to control whether play buttons are displayed on cards containing audio.

## Changes Applied

### 1. String Resources (`AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/10-preferences.xml`)
- Added `show_audio_play_buttons` string resource with title: "Show play buttons on cards with audio"
- Added `show_audio_play_buttons_summ` string resource with summary: "Show or hide play buttons on the card content"

### 2. Preference Definition (`AnkiDroid/src/main/res/xml/preferences_appearance.xml`)
- Updated the `show_audio_play_buttons_key` SwitchPreferenceCompat to:
  - Use proper `android:title` and `android:summary` attributes referencing the string resources
  - Kept `android:defaultValue="true"` for backward compatibility (play buttons shown by default)

### 3. Sound Processing Logic (`AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/SoundKt.kt`)
- Modified `addPlayIcons()` function to accept a `showIcons: Boolean = true` parameter
- Added logic to skip adding play buttons when `showIcons` is false
- This prevents the play button HTML from being inserted when the preference is disabled

### 4. Sound Expansion (`AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/Sound.kt`)
- Modified `expandSounds()` companion method to accept a `showIcons: Boolean = true` parameter
- Passes the parameter through to `addPlayIcons()`

### 5. Card Appearance (`AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/CardAppearance.kt`)
- Added `showAudioPlayButtons: Boolean` property to the class constructor
- Modified `create()` method to read the preference from SharedPreferences
- Stores the preference value for use when rendering cards
- Default value is `true` to maintain backward compatibility

### 6. HTML Generation (`AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/HtmlGenerator.kt`)
- Modified `expandSounds()` method to pass the `showAudioPlayButtons` preference value
- Connects the stored preference to the actual rendering logic

### 7. Preference Handler (`AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/preferences/AppearanceSettingsFragment.kt`)
- Added preference handler for `show_audio_play_buttons_key`
- Reads the current value from SharedPreferences on load
- Saves changes to SharedPreferences when user toggles the preference
- Default value is `true` (show play buttons)

### 8. Analytics (`AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/analytics/UsageAnalytics.kt`)
- Added `"showAudioPlayButtons"` to the `preferencesWhoseChangesShouldBeReported` set
- Enables tracking of how users are using this feature

## How It Works

1. **Preference Storage**: The user's choice is stored in Android SharedPreferences under the key `"showAudioPlayButtons"` with default value `true`.

2. **Reading the Preference**: When `CardAppearance.create()` is called, it reads the preference value and stores it in the `CardAppearance` instance.

3. **Card Rendering**: When cards are rendered:
   - `CardHtml.getContent()` calls `HtmlGenerator.expandSounds()`
   - `HtmlGenerator.expandSounds()` calls `Sound.expandSounds()` with the `showAudioPlayButtons` value
   - `Sound.expandSounds()` calls `addPlayIcons()` with the same value
   - `addPlayIcons()` checks the value and either returns the content unchanged (false) or adds play button HTML (true)

4. **User Control**: Users can toggle the preference in the Appearance settings screen. The change takes effect immediately for newly rendered cards.

## Backward Compatibility

- Default value is `true` (show play buttons), matching the existing behavior
- No breaking changes to existing functionality
- The preference is opt-in for users who want to hide play buttons

## Testing Recommendations

1. Verify the preference appears in Appearance settings
2. Test toggling the preference off/on
3. Confirm cards with audio show/hide play buttons based on the preference
4. Verify the preference persists across app restarts
5. Test that audio still plays when play buttons are hidden (users can still use hardware controls or gestures)
6. Verify analytics are tracking preference changes

## Files Modified

1. `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/10-preferences.xml` - String resources
2. `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/xml/preferences_appearance.xml` - Preference UI definition
3. `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/SoundKt.kt` - Play icon generation logic
4. `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/Sound.kt` - Sound expansion logic
5. `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/CardAppearance.kt` - Preference storage
6. `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/HtmlGenerator.kt` - HTML generation logic
7. `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/preferences/AppearanceSettingsFragment.kt` - Preference UI handling
8. `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/analytics/UsageAnalytics.kt` - Analytics tracking

## Implementation Notes

- The preference is stored in app-level SharedPreferences rather than collection config because it's a UI/display preference, not a deck/collection-level setting
- The implementation follows the existing pattern used by other preferences (e.g., `centerVertically`, `cardZoom`, etc.)
- The change is minimal and targeted, affecting only the display of play buttons without modifying audio playback functionality"}

content.text:
Perfect! I have successfully implemented the "Show play buttons on cards with audio" preference feature for AnkiDroid. 

## Summary

The implementation adds a preference setting that matches Anki Desktop's functionality, allowing users to control whether play buttons are displayed on cards containing audio.

### What Was Implemented

1. **String Resources** - Added title and summary strings for the preference in `10-preferences.xml`
2. **Preference UI** - Updated `preferences_appearance.xml` to show the preference properly with a title and summary
3. **Core Logic** - Modified the sound processing chain (`SoundKt.kt` and `Sound.kt`) to conditionally add play icons based on the preference
4. **State Management** - Updated `CardAppearance.kt` to store and retrieve the preference value
5. **Integration** - Modified `HtmlGenerator.kt` to pass the preference through to the rendering logic
6. **UI Handler** - Added preference handler in `AppearanceSettingsFragment.kt` to read/write user's choice
7. **Analytics** - Added tracking in `UsageAnalytics.kt` to monitor feature usage

### Key Features

- ✅ Default value is `true` (show play buttons) for backward compatibility
- ✅ Preference stored in app-level SharedPreferences
- ✅ Changes take effect immediately for newly rendered cards
- ✅ Analytics tracking enabled
- ✅ All files updated and verified

### How It Works

The preference flows through the card rendering pipeline:
1. User toggles preference in Appearance settings
2. Preference is saved to SharedPreferences
3. When card renders, `CardAppearance` retrieves the preference
4. `HtmlGenerator.expandSounds()` passes it to `Sound.expandSounds()`
5. `addPlayIcons()` uses it to decide whether to insert play button HTML

The implementation is complete, minimal, and follows the existing codebase patterns!

If not, see . 15→ * 16→ * This file incorporates code under the following license 17→ * https://github.com/ankitects/anki/blob/2.1.34/pylib/anki/sound.py 18→ * 19→ * Copyright: Ankitects Pty Ltd and contributors 20→ * License: GNU AGPL, version 3 or later; http://www.gnu.org/licenses/agpl.html 21→ */ 22→ 23→@file:Suppress("unused") 24→ 25→package com.ichi2.libanki 26→ 27→import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager.withCol 28→import com.ichi2.utils.KotlinCleanup 29→ 30→@KotlinCleanup("combine file with Sound.kt if done in libAnki") 31→/** 32→ * Records information about a text to speech tag. 33→ */ 34→data class TTSTag( 35→ val fieldText: String, 36→ /** 37→ * Language may be empty if coming from AnkiDroid reading the whole card 38→ */ 39→ val lang: String, 40→ val voices: List, 41→ val speed: Float, 42→ /** each arg should be in the form 'foo=bar' */ 43→ val otherArgs: List 44→) : AvTag() 45→ 46→/** 47→ * Contains the filename inside a [sound:...] tag. 48→ * 49→ * Video files also use [sound:...]. 50→ */ 51→data class SoundOrVideoTag(val filename: String) : AvTag() 52→ 53→/** In python, this is a union of [TTSTag] and [SoundOrVideoTag] */ 54→open class AvTag 55→ 56→/* Methods */ 57→ 58→val AV_REF_RE = Regex("\\[anki:(play:(.):(\\d+))]") 59→val AV_PLAYLINK_RE = Regex("playsound:(.):(\\d+)") 60→ 61→fun stripAvRefs(text: String) = AV_REF_RE.replace("", text) 62→ 63→fun addPlayIcons(content: String): String { 64→ return AV_REF_RE.replace(content) { match -> 65→ val groups = match.groupValues 66→ val side = groups[2] 67→ val index = groups[3] 68→ val playsound = "playsound:$side:$index" 69→ """ 70→ 72→ """ 73→ } 74→} 75→ 76→/** Extract av tag from playsound:q:x link */ 77→suspend fun getAvTag(card: Card, url: String): AvTag? { 78→ return AV_PLAYLINK_RE.matchEntire(url)?.let { 79→ val values = it.groupValues 80→ val questionSide = values[1] == "q" 81→ val index = values[2].toInt() 82→ val tags = withCol { 83→ if (questionSide) { card.questionAvTags() } else { card.answerAvTags() } 84→ } 85→ if (index < tags.size) { 86→ tags[index] 87→ } else { 88→ null 89→ } 90→ } 91→} 92→

 23→<!-- Fonts & Style Preferences -->
 24→<PreferenceScreen xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"
 25→    xmlns:app="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res-auto"
 26→    xmlns:search="http://schemas.android.com/apk/com.bytehamster.lib.preferencesearch"

 28→        android:title="@string/pref_cat_appearance"

 30→    <PreferenceCategory android:title="@string/pref_cat_themes" >
 31→        <ListPreference
 32→            android:defaultValue="0"
 33→            android:entries="@array/app_theme_labels"
 34→            android:entryValues="@array/app_theme_values"
 35→            android:key="@string/app_theme_key"
